evals.prompt: I'm starting a new C++ command-line tool called 'image_tool' on my MacBook Pro (Apple Silicon). I want a modern setup with CMake, vcpkg, unit tests, formatting, linting, and VS Code debugging. Create the project scaffold in /tmp/cpp_eval_image_tool.
evals.expected_output: A greenfield C++ project at /tmp/cpp_eval_image_tool with CMakeLists.txt, CMakePresets.json, vcpkg.json, src/main.cpp, library target, GoogleTest tests, .clang-format, .clang-tidy, .vscode settings/launch, and README tuned for macOS arm64.
expectations: CMakeLists.txt sets CXX standard to 20 and exports compile commands
vcpkg.json includes gtest as a dependency
CMakePresets.json contains macos-arm64-debug and macos-arm64-tidy presets
A library target image_tool_lib and executable image_tool_app are defined
.vscode/launch.json has a CodeLLDB debug configuration for the app
README.md references the macOS Apple Silicon host profile and bootstrap command

evals.prompt: I have an existing C++ project in /tmp/cpp_eval_legacy that only has a Makefile and a single src/main.cpp with a hello-world program. Modernize it using the C++ project setup skill: switch to CMake + vcpkg, add a reusable library, GoogleTest tests, clang-format, clang-tidy, and VS Code settings. Preserve the existing hello-world behavior.
evals.expected_output: The existing project is converted to CMake/vcpkg while keeping the original hello-world behavior, with new tests and quality tooling added.
expectations: The original src/main.cpp hello-world logic is preserved or moved without losing behavior
CMakeLists.txt is created and references a library target
tests/ directory with at least one GoogleTest file is added
README.md explains how to build and test the modernized project

evals.prompt: Create a cross-platform C++20 library project named 'math_utils' for Windows x64 in /tmp/cpp_eval_math_utils. It should be a static library with unit tests, clang-tidy analysis preset, and PowerShell format/bootstrap scripts. Also add a VS Code launch config for cppvsdbg.
evals.expected_output: A Windows-x64-oriented C++20 library scaffold with math_utils_lib, tests, windows-x64 presets, PowerShell scripts, and cppvsdbg launch configuration.
expectations: CMakeLists.txt targets C++20 and defines a math_utils_lib library
CMakePresets.json contains windows-x64-debug, windows-x64-release, and windows-x64-tidy presets
.vscode/launch.json includes a cppvsdbg configuration for Windows
README.md shows PowerShell bootstrap and build commands
